LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARY KAHN'S MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION OF LIMITED CIVIL JURISDICTION CASE TO UNLIMITED CIVIL JURISDICTION CASE BY THIS PLEADING. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE GOOD CAUSE UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473 BASED ON THE HOLDING IN ZAMORA V CLAYBORN CONTRACTING (2002) 28 CAL. 4TH 249. THE DETERMINATION OF THE APPROPRIATE JURISDICTION TO FILE THE ACTION INVOLVES THE PROFESSIONAL JUDGMENT OF COUNSEL. IT IS NOT A DECISION FAIRLY IMPUTABLE TO THE CLIENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

APPEARANCES: ERIN BERNSTEIN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3975) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.

THE DETERMINATION OF THE APPROPRIATE JURISDICTION TO FILE THE ACTION INVOLVES THE PROFESSIONAL JUDGMENT OF COUNSEL. IT IS NOT A DECISION FAIRLY IMPUTABLE TO THE CLIENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
